UX Impact: Coherent workspace across views.
Notes: Consolidates view behavior under one Stage system.

Business Description: Switching views should not feel like a hard reset; the workspace should keep useful context so users do not lose their place.
Functional Detail: View switching preserves useful context where possible: selected task, focus state, expanded containers, and stage position. Transitions provide feedback rather than reloading the workspace.
Affected Modules: src/pages/builder/stage/; view renderers; preference layer
UX Impact: Smooth, continuous view changes.
Notes: Context preservation is best-effort and safe.

Business Description: Users dragging items in Kanban must never lose drag context when the target is outside the visible area.
Functional Detail: Kanban movement: phases horizontal; actions vertical within a phase and horizontal between phases; tasks within and between actions. When dragging near/beyond visible bounds, support edge-scroll, valid insertion zones, off-stage dropzones, and continuation feedback (e.g. moving a phase beyond bounds shows a valid insertion area or scrolls; moving an action to a far phase triggers horizontal movement/target highlight; moving a task to an action highlights the destination).
Affected Modules: src/pages/builder/kanban/; stage movement; dropzone engine
UX Impact: Reliable long-distance drag in Kanban.
Notes: User never loses drag context off-screen.

Business Description: Timeline planning must support dragging tasks to dates outside the visible range without forcing the user to cancel and restart.
Functional Detail: Timeline movement: next/previous week or month moves the stage in a controlled way; dragging a task toward a date outside the visible range exposes edge navigation or valid outside-date dropzones; dropping on a valid date updates the task's date context.
Affected Modules: src/pages/builder/timeline/; stage movement; dropzone engine
UX Impact: Fluid timeline scheduling.
Notes: Supports drag across time ranges without restart.

Business Description: Different surfaces affect the Stage differently, and this must be explicit so future components do not break the canvas.
Functional Detail: Explicit layout contract: Editor/Viewer Island reduces/pushes the stage (no overlay); sticky popup shell floats above the stage (no resize); Focus Island filters/isolates visible data (no mutation); View Helper popup floats above the stage and bridges opposite-view logic. Future components must declare which contract they follow.
Affected Modules: src/pages/builder/stage/StageManager.tsx; island layout contract
UX Impact: Predictable layout as features grow.
Notes: Foundational contract for all builder surfaces.

Business Description: Dropzones should be generated by view rules, not hardcoded inside components, so Kanban and Timeline drag/drop stay consistent and future-proof.
Functional Detail: A typed dropzone model defines: view type, target type, accepted dragged object types, target ID, position, active state, edge-zone state, and the command to run on drop. Dropzones are generated by view rules. Drop actions route through named mutation commands.
Affected Modules: dropzone engine; src/pages/builder/kanban/; src/pages/builder/timeline/; builder mutation commands
UX Impact: Consistent, reliable drag/drop.
Notes: Guardrail: drag/drop routes through named mutation commands.
